Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
An online mathematical encyclopedia devoted to Deriving and exploring mathematics beyond the classroom for high school students. All Things Are Derivable

Our mission is to make mathematical reasoning transparent, tangible, and telling through Derivation while offering additional pathways for inquisitive and motivated students to explore mathematics with greter rigor and depth.
This project aims to demystify mathematical Deriving and to foster deeper curiosity through clear and careful reasoning.

Throughout this project, Derivation is treated as a proper noun, referring to the process of obtain a result through meticulous, step-by-step calculation. As the main theme of Art of Derivation, all words related to Derivation (such as Derive, Deriving) are highlighted in the main theme color. Some closely related terms, though not strictly equivalent, including Proof, Justification, and Logical Argument, all of which are integral to the idea of Derivation presented in this project. My understanding of Derivation was mainly developed from my AP Physics C: Mechanics course, where the Free Response Questions frequently require student to Derive expressions from fundamental definitions or equations in the provided equations sheet.
This project is built on Docusaurus and deployed on GitHub Pages. Below are detailed format information.

All documents are stored as Markdown files and use either the .md or .mdx extension. All document file names are capitalized and connected using hyphens (-). All documents are stored under the docs directory, organized by sidebars.js. All referenced images (such as .jpg, .png, .svg) are stored in the static/img directory.
All documents start with the front matter to ensure the consistent format and navigation. More additional details, refer to Markdown Features | Docusaurus.
All mathematical expressions are rendered using KaTeX with remark-math and follow standard LaTeX\LaTeXLATEвАЛX syntax.

All Derivation processes are placed in collapsible blocks, implemented using <details>+<summary>. All Derivation processes are organized using the align* enviornment. All Derivation processes are completed with \blacksquare (вЦ†\blacksquareвЦ†), indicating Q.E.D. (quod erat demonstrandum), meaning "that which was to be demonstrated".

Note The Gray Admonition provides extra and unnecessary notes and information. Definition The Green Admonition provides definition about the given topic. Property, Identity, Solution... The Blue Admonition provides Properties, Identities, Solutions, and so on that is NOT designed to show the collapsable Derivation process. Derivation The collapsable Admonition provides detailed process for Derivation Question The Orange Admonition highlights the question for examples. Warning The Red Admonition provides information that is important to know.

The theme color of this project is Indigo (#4b0082) in light mode and Magenta (#ff61ff) in dark mode. The theme color is exclusively reserved for Derivation and its related concepts, rather than decorative styling.
The idea of the favicon came from вИµ\becauseвИµ ($\because$) and вИі\thereforeвИі ($\therefore$).

All documents are organized in a logical sequence; following the given order is strongly recommended. Before viewing the full derivation through the collapsable bar, attempt to work through each step independently using pen and paper. Detailed solutions are provided in the collapsible sections for verification and deeper understanding. Maintaining a dedicated notebook for your own derivations is highly encouraged.

This page lists several fundamental integrals and their basic properties. Their Derivations are mostly straightforward, as they follow directly from reversing those common derivatives.

This page will explain Integral in a nut shell,

Below are 147 advanced integral formulas listed in the appendix of the Higher Mathematics (Tongji University).Each formula is Derived step by step. It may seem futile and impractival to memorize all the formulas, but it is a great opportunity to consolidate integration techniques and appreciate heuristics behind them.

In my AP Physics C Mechanics class, many students were struggling to Derive the terminal velocity of a free-falling object, and the primary reason was that they can't solve the Integral Formula #21. My teacher back then found a really sophisticated way through hyperbolic trigonometry substitution, and everyone was stunned by the complexity, but I came up with a simple algebraic transformation. An example is below: Solution Let: u=x3u=x^3u=x3 dudx=3x2du=3x2 dx\begin{align*} \frac{\mathrm{d}u}{\mathrm{d}x}&=3x^2\\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}&=\color{red}{3x^2\ \mathrm{d}x}\\ \end{align*}dxduвАЛduвАЛ=3x2=3x2 dxвАЛ Plug in back to the question: вИЂ3x2sinвБ°x3 dx=вИЂsinвБ°u du=вИЂsinвБ°u du=вИТcosвБ°u+C=вИТcosвБ°x3+C\begin{align*} \int{\color{red}{3x^2}}\sin{x^3}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}x}&=\int{\sin{u}}\ \color{red}{\mathrm{d}u}\\ &=\int{\sin{u}}\ \mathrm{d}u\\ &=-\cos{u}+C\\ &=-\cos{x^3}+C \end{align*}вИЂ3x2sinx3 dxвАЛ=вИЂsinu du=вИЂsinu du=вИТcosu+C=вИТcosx3+CвАЛ Hence: вИЂ3x2sinвБ°x3 dx=вИТcosвБ°x3+CвЦ†\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=-\cos{x^3}+C \tag*{$\blacksquare$}вИЂ3x2sinx3 dx=вИТcosx3+CвЦ† By replacing the variable xxx into a function of uuu, we can simplify the overall question to a common integral. Direct Substitution is a method created by the author himself. It simplify the process of substitution without write out uuu and du\mathrm{d}udu. I will show this with an example: вИЂ3x2sinвБ°x3 dx= ?\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\ ?вИЂ3x2sinx3 dx= ? This time, let's try what I called Direct Substituton: Direct Substitution Approach After analyze, we want to substitute x3x^3x3. All we need to do is substitude dx\mathrm{d}xdx to a fraction where the top is dx3\mathrm{d}x^3dx3 and the bottom is the derivative of x3x^3x3 based on xxx, as: вИЂ3x2sinвБ°x3 dx=вИЂ3x2sinвБ°x3 d(x3)3x2=вИЂsinвБ°x3 d(x3)\int{3x^2\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{\cancel{3x^2}\sin{x^3}}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(x^3)}{\cancel{3x^2}}=\int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)вИЂ3x2sinx3 dx=вИЂ3x2sinx3 3x2d(x3)вАЛ=вИЂsinx3 d(x3) And all we need is to consider x3x^3x3 as uuu and integrate normally: вЯє вИЂsinвБ°x3 d(x3)=вИТcosвБ°x3+C\implies \int{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}+CвЯєвИЂsinx3 d(x3)=вИТcosx3+C More importantly, for definite integral, it doesn't need to change bounds, as: вИЂab3x2sinвБ°x3 dx=вИЂabsinвБ°x3 d(x3)=вИТcosвБ°x3вИ£ab=вИТcosвБ°b3+cosвБ°a3\int_{a}^{b}3x^2\sin{x^3}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int_{a}^{b}{\sin{x^3}}\ \mathrm{d}(x^3)=-\cos{x^3}\big|_{a}^{b}=-\cos{b^3}+\cos{a^3}вИЂabвАЛ3x2sinx3 dx=вИЂabвАЛsinx3 d(x3)=вИТcosx3вАЛabвАЛ=вИТcosb3+cosa3 Direct Substitution In general, if we want to substitude ax+bax+bax+b or term that can cancel the other one, we may use direct substitution вИЂf(x)g(x)вПЯSubstitute dx=вИЂf(x)g(x) d(g(x))gвА≤(x)\int{{f(x)}\underbrace{g(x)}_{\text{Substitute}}}\ \mathrm{d}x=\int{f(x)g(x)}\ \frac{\mathrm{d}(g(x))}{g'(x)} вИЂf(x)Substituteg(x)вАЛвАЛ dx=вИЂf(x)g(x) gвА≤(x)d(g(x))вАЛ

From the Pythagorean Theorem, we get: a2вИТx2=b2вИТ(cвИТx)2a2вИТx2=b2вИТc2+2cxвИТx22cx=a2вИТb2+c2x=a2вИТb2+c22c\begin{align*} a^2-x^2&=b^2-(c-x)^2\\ a^2-x^2&=b^2-c^2+2cx-x^2\\ 2cx&=a^2-b^2+c^2\\ x&=\frac{a^2-b^2+c^2}{2c} \end{align*}a2вИТx2a2вИТx22cxxвАЛ=b2вИТ(cвИТx)2=b2вИТc2+2cxвИТx2=a2вИТb2+c2=2ca2вИТb2+c2вАЛвАЛ SвЦ≥ABC=12ACвЛЕBH=12aвЛЕc2вИТ(a2вИТb2+c22c)2=14[a2c2вИТ(a2вИТb2+c22)2](Qin JiushaoвАЩs formula)=116[4a2c2вИТ(a2вИТb2+c2)2]=(2ac)2вИТ(a2вИТb2+c2)216=(2ac+a2вИТb2+c2)(2acвИТa2+b2вИТc2)16=[(a+c)2вИТb2][b2вИТ(aвИТc)2]16=(a+c+b)(a+cвИТb)(b+aвИТc)(bвИТa+c)16=a+b+c2вИТa+b+c2aвИТb+c2a+bвИТc2=a+b+c2(a+b+c2вИТa)(a+b+c2вИТb)(a+b+c2вИТc) \begin{align*} S_{\triangle{ABC}}&=\frac{1}{2}AC\cdot BH \\ &=\frac{1}{2}a\cdot \sqrt{c^2-\left(\frac{a^2-b^2+c^2}{2c}\right)^2}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{1}{4}\left[a^2c^2-\left(\frac{a^2-b^2+c^2}{2}\right)^2 \right]} &&\text{(Qin Jiushao's formula)}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{1}{16}[4a^2c^2-(a^2-b^2+c^2)^2]}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{(2ac)^2-(a^2-b^2+c^2)^2}{16}}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{(2ac+a^2-b^2+c^2)(2ac-a^2+b^2-c^2)}{16}}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{[(a+c)^2-b^2][b^2-(a-c)^2]}{16}}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{(a+c+b)(a+c-b)(b+a-c)(b-a+c)}{16}}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{a+b+c}{2}\frac{-a+b+c}{2}\frac{a-b+c}{2}\frac{a+b-c}{2}}\\ &=\sqrt{\frac{a+b+c}{2}\left(\frac{a+b+c}{2}-a\right)\left(\frac{a+b+c}{2}-b\right)\left(\frac{a+b+c}{2}-c\right)}\\ \end{align*}SвЦ≥ABCвАЛвАЛ=21вАЛACвЛЕBH=21вАЛaвЛЕc2вИТ(2ca2вИТb2+c2вАЛ)2вАЛ=41вАЛ[a2c2вИТ(2a2вИТb2+c2вАЛ)2]вАЛ=161вАЛ[4a2c2вИТ(a2вИТb2+c2)2]вАЛ=16(2ac)2вИТ(a2вИТb2+c2)2вАЛвАЛ=16(2ac+a2вИТb2+c2)(2acвИТa2+b2вИТc2)вАЛвАЛ=16[(a+c)2вИТb2][b2вИТ(aвИТc)2]вАЛвАЛ=16(a+c+b)(a+cвИТb)(b+aвИТc)(bвИТa+c)вАЛвАЛ=2a+b+cвАЛ2вИТa+b+cвАЛ2aвИТb+cвАЛ2a+bвИТcвАЛвАЛ=2a+b+cвАЛ(2a+b+cвАЛвИТa)(2a+b+cвАЛвИТb)(2a+b+cвАЛвИТc)вАЛвАЛ(Qin JiushaoвАЩs formula) Now, let p=a+b+c2p=\frac{a+b+c}{2}p=2a+b+cвАЛ, then: SвЦ≥ABC=p(pвИТa)(pвИТb)(pвИТc)вЦ† S_{\triangle{ABC}}=\sqrt{p(p-a)(p-b)(p-c)} \tag*{$\blacksquare$}SвЦ≥ABCвАЛ=p(pвИТa)(pвИТb)(pвИТc)вАЛвЦ†

sinвБ°(ќ±+ќ≤)=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤+cosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤\sin{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\sin{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\cos{\alpha}\sin{\beta}sin(ќ±+ќ≤)=sinќ±cosќ≤+cosќ±sinќ≤ Derivation sinвБ°ќ≤=BCAB=BC1 вЯє BC=sinвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ≤=ACAB=AC1 вЯє AC=cosвБ°ќ≤sinвБ°ќ±=CDAC=CDcosвБ°ќ≤ вЯє CD=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ±=CFBC=CFsinвБ°ќ≤ вЯє CF=cosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinќ≤=ABBCвАЛ=1BCвАЛвЯєBCcosќ≤=ABACвАЛ=1ACвАЛвЯєACsinќ±=ACCDвАЛ=cosќ≤CDвАЛвЯєCDcosќ±=BCCFвАЛ=sinќ≤CFвАЛвЯєCFвАЛ=sinќ≤=cosќ≤=sinќ±cosќ≤=cosќ±sinќ≤вАЛsinвБ°(ќ±+ќ≤)=BEAB=BE1=DF=CD+CF=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤+cosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \sin{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BE}{AB}=\frac{BE}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin(ќ±+ќ≤)вАЛ=ABBEвАЛ=1BEвАЛ=DF=CD+CF=sinќ±cosќ≤+cosќ±sinќ≤вАЛвЦ†вАЛ

sinвБ°(ќ±вИТќ≤)=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤вИТcosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤\sin{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\sin{\alpha}\cos{\beta}-\cos{\alpha}\sin{\beta}sin(ќ±вИТќ≤)=sinќ±cosќ≤вИТcosќ±sinќ≤ Derivation sinвБ°ќ≤=BCAB=BC1 вЯє BC=sinвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ≤=ACAB=AC1 вЯє AC=cosвБ°ќ≤sinвБ°ќ±=ADAC=ADcosвБ°ќ≤ вЯє AD=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ±=BFBC=BFsinвБ°ќ≤ вЯє BF=cosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{AC}=\frac{AD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies AD&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{BC}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinќ≤=ABBCвАЛ=1BCвАЛвЯєBCcosќ≤=ABACвАЛ=1ACвАЛвЯєACsinќ±=ACADвАЛ=cosќ≤ADвАЛвЯєADcosќ±=BCBFвАЛ=sinќ≤BFвАЛвЯєBFвАЛ=sinќ≤=cosќ≤=sinќ±cosќ≤=cosќ±sinќ≤вАЛsinвБ°(ќ±вИТќ≤)=BGAB=BG1=AE=ADвИТED=ADвИТBF=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤вИТcosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \sin{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BG}{AB}=\frac{BG}{1}=AE\\ &=AD-ED\\ &=AD-BF\\ &=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}-\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin(ќ±вИТќ≤)вАЛ=ABBGвАЛ=1BGвАЛ=AE=ADвИТED=ADвИТBF=sinќ±cosќ≤вИТcosќ±sinќ≤вАЛвЦ†вАЛ

cosвБ°(ќ±+ќ≤)=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤вИТsinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤\cos{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}-\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(ќ±+ќ≤)=cosќ±cosќ≤вИТsinќ±sinќ≤ Derivation sinвБ°ќ≤=BCAB=BC1 вЯє BC=sinвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ≤=ACAB=AC1 вЯє AC=cosвБ°ќ≤sinвБ°ќ±=BFAC=BFsinвБ°ќ≤ вЯє BF=sinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ±=ADBC=ADcosвБ°ќ≤ вЯє AD=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{AC}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{BC}=\frac{AD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies AD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinќ≤=ABBCвАЛ=1BCвАЛвЯєBCcosќ≤=ABACвАЛ=1ACвАЛвЯєACsinќ±=ACBFвАЛ=sinќ≤BFвАЛвЯєBFcosќ±=BCADвАЛ=cosќ≤ADвАЛвЯєADвАЛ=sinќ≤=cosќ≤=sinќ±sinќ≤=cosќ±cosќ≤вАЛcosвБ°(ќ±+ќ≤)=AEAB=AE1=ADвИТED=ADвИТBF=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤вИТsinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AE}{AB}=\frac{AE}{1}\\ &=AD-ED\\ &=AD-BF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}-\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(ќ±+ќ≤)вАЛ=ABAEвАЛ=1AEвАЛ=ADвИТED=ADвИТBF=cosќ±cosќ≤вИТsinќ±sinќ≤вАЛвЦ†вАЛ

cosвБ°(ќ±вИТќ≤)=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤+sinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤\cos{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\cos{\alpha}\cos{\beta}+\sin{\alpha}\sin{\beta}cos(ќ±вИТќ≤)=cosќ±cosќ≤+sinќ±sinќ≤ Derivation sinвБ°ќ≤=BCAB=BC1 вЯє BC=sinвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ≤=ACAB=AC1 вЯє AC=cosвБ°ќ≤sinвБ°ќ±=CFBC=CFsinвБ°ќ≤ вЯє CF=sinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤cosвБ°ќ±=CDAC=CDcosвБ°ќ≤ вЯє CD=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \blue{\sin{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AB}=\frac{BC}{1}\implies BC&=\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \blue{\cos{\beta}}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \green{\sin{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BC}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\sin{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}}\\ \green{\cos{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AC}=\frac{CD}{\blue{\cos{\beta}}}\implies CD&=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}\\ \end{align*}sinќ≤=ABBCвАЛ=1BCвАЛвЯєBCcosќ≤=ABACвАЛ=1ACвАЛвЯєACsinќ±=BCCFвАЛ=sinќ≤CFвАЛвЯєCFcosќ±=ACCDвАЛ=cosќ≤CDвАЛвЯєCDвАЛ=sinќ≤=cosќ≤=sinќ±sinќ≤=cosќ±cosќ≤вАЛcosвБ°(ќ±вИТќ≤)=AGAB=AG1=DF=CD+CF=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ≤+sinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \cos{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{AG}{AB}=\frac{AG}{1}=DF\\ &=CD+CF\\ &=\green{\cos{\alpha}}\blue{\cos{\beta}}+\green{\sin{\alpha}}\blue{\sin{\beta}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos(ќ±вИТќ≤)вАЛ=ABAGвАЛ=1AGвАЛ=DF=CD+CF=cosќ±cosќ≤+sinќ±sinќ≤вАЛвЦ†вАЛ

tanвБ°(ќ±+ќ≤)=tanвБ°ќ±+tanвБ°ќ≤1вИТtanвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤\tan{\left(\alpha+\beta\right)}=\frac{\tan{\alpha}+\tan{\beta}}{1-\tan{\alpha}\tan{\beta}}tan(ќ±+ќ≤)=1вИТtanќ±tanќ≤tanќ±+tanќ≤вАЛ Derivation tanвБ°ќ±=CDAD=CD1 вЯє CD=tanвБ°ќ±secвБ°ќ±=ACAD=AC1 вЯє AC=secвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤=BCAC=BCsecвБ°ќ± вЯє BC=secвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤secвБ°ќ±=BCCF=secвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤CF вЯє CF=tanвБ°ќ≤tanвБ°ќ±=BFCF=BFtanвБ°ќ≤ вЯє BF=tanвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{CD}{AD}=\frac{CD}{1}\implies CD&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{AC}{AD}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\\ \blue{\tan{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{BC}{\green{\sec{\alpha}}}\implies BC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{BC}{CF}=\frac{\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{CF}\implies CF&=\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{BF}{CF}=\frac{BF}{\blue{\tan{\beta}}}\implies BF&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}} \end{align*}tanќ±=ADCDвАЛ=1CDвАЛвЯєCDsecќ±=ADACвАЛ=1ACвАЛвЯєACtanќ≤=ACBCвАЛ=secќ±BCвАЛвЯєBCsecќ±=CFBCвАЛ=CFsecќ±tanќ≤вАЛвЯєCFtanќ±=CFBFвАЛ=tanќ≤BFвАЛвЯєBFвАЛ=tanќ±=secќ±=secќ±tanќ≤=tanќ≤=tanќ±tanќ≤вАЛtanвБ°(ќ±+ќ≤)=BEAE=DFAE=CD+CFADвИТBF=tanвБ°ќ±+tanвБ°ќ≤1вИТtanвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \tan{(\green{\alpha}+\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BE}{AE}=\frac{DF}{AE}=\frac{CD+CF}{AD-BF}\\ &=\frac{\green{\tan{\alpha}}+\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{1-\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}tan(ќ±+ќ≤)вАЛ=AEBEвАЛ=AEDFвАЛ=ADвИТBFCD+CFвАЛ=1вИТtanќ±tanќ≤tanќ±+tanќ≤вАЛвАЛвЦ†вАЛ

tanвБ°(ќ±вИТќ≤)=tanвБ°ќ±вИТtanвБ°ќ≤1+tanвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤\tan{\left(\alpha-\beta\right)}=\frac{\tan{\alpha}-\tan{\beta}}{1+\tan{\alpha}\tan{\beta}}tan(ќ±вИТќ≤)=1+tanќ±tanќ≤tanќ±вИТtanќ≤вАЛ Derivation tanвБ°ќ±=ADCD=AD1 вЯє AD=tanвБ°ќ±secвБ°ќ±=ACCD=AC1 вЯє AC=secвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤=BCAC=BCsecвБ°ќ± вЯє BC=secвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤secвБ°ќ±=BCBF=secвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤BF вЯє BF=tanвБ°ќ≤tanвБ°ќ±=CFBF=CFtanвБ°ќ≤ вЯє CF=tanвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{AD}{CD}=\frac{AD}{1}\implies AD&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{AC}{CD}=\frac{AC}{1}\implies AC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\\ \blue{\tan{\beta}}=\frac{BC}{AC}=\frac{BC}{\green{\sec{\alpha}}}\implies BC&=\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\sec{\alpha}}=\frac{BC}{BF}=\frac{\green{\sec{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{BF}\implies BF&=\blue{\tan{\beta}}\\ \green{\tan{\alpha}}=\frac{CF}{BF}=\frac{CF}{\blue{\tan{\beta}}}\implies CF&=\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}} \end{align*}tanќ±=CDADвАЛ=1ADвАЛвЯєADsecќ±=CDACвАЛ=1ACвАЛвЯєACtanќ≤=ACBCвАЛ=secќ±BCвАЛвЯєBCsecќ±=BFBCвАЛ=BFsecќ±tanќ≤вАЛвЯєBFtanќ±=BFCFвАЛ=tanќ≤CFвАЛвЯєCFвАЛ=tanќ±=secќ±=secќ±tanќ≤=tanќ≤=tanќ±tanќ≤вАЛtanвБ°(ќ±вИТќ≤)=BGAG=AEDF=ADвИТBFCD+CF=tanвБ°ќ±вИТtanвБ°ќ≤1+tanвБ°ќ±tanвБ°ќ≤ \begin{align*} \tan{(\green{\alpha}-\blue{\beta})}&=\frac{BG}{AG}=\frac{AE}{DF}=\frac{AD-BF}{CD+CF}\\ &=\frac{\green{\tan{\alpha}}-\blue{\tan{\beta}}}{1+\green{\tan{\alpha}}\blue{\tan{\beta}}} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}tan(ќ±вИТќ≤)вАЛ=AGBGвАЛ=DFAEвАЛ=CD+CFADвИТBFвАЛ=1+tanќ±tanќ≤tanќ±вИТtanќ≤вАЛвАЛвЦ†вАЛ

sinвБ°2ќ±=2sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ±\sin{2\alpha}=2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha}sin2ќ±=2sinќ±cosќ± Derivation sinвБ°2ќ±=sinвБ°(ќ±+ќ±)=sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ±+cosвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ±(Trig Identity #4)=2sinвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ±\begin{align*} \sin{2\alpha}&=\sin{({\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\sin{{\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}+\cos{{\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#4}})\\ &=2\sin{\alpha}\cos{\alpha} \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}sin2ќ±вАЛ=sin(ќ±+ќ±)=sinќ±cosќ±+cosќ±sinќ±=2sinќ±cosќ±вАЛвАЛ(Trig Identity #4)вАЛвЦ†вАЛ Trig Identity #4

cosвБ°2ќ±=cosвБ°2ќ±вИТsinвБ°2ќ±=2cosвБ°2ќ±вИТ1=1вИТ2sinвБ°2ќ±\cos{2\alpha}=\cos^2{\alpha}-\sin^2{\alpha}=2\cos^2{\alpha}-1=1-2\sin^2{\alpha}cos2ќ±=cos2ќ±вИТsin2ќ±=2cos2ќ±вИТ1=1вИТ2sin2ќ± Derivation cosвБ°2ќ±=cosвБ°(ќ±+ќ±)=cosвБ°ќ±cosвБ°ќ±вИТsinвБ°ќ±sinвБ°ќ±(Trig Identity #6)=cosвБ°2ќ±вИТsinвБ°2ќ±=cosвБ°2ќ±вИТ(1вИТcosвБ°2ќ±)=2cosвБ°2ќ±вИТ1(Trig Identity #1)=(1вИТsinвБ°2ќ±)вИТsinвБ°2ќ±=1вИТ2sinвБ°2ќ±(Trig Identity #1)\begin{align*} \cos{2\alpha}&=\cos{({\alpha}+{\alpha})}\\ &=\cos{{\alpha}}\cos{{\alpha}}-\sin{{\alpha}}\sin{{\alpha}} &&({\text{Trig Identity \#6}})\\ &=\cos^2{\alpha}-\sin^2{\alpha}\\ &=\cos^2{\alpha}-(1-\cos^2{\alpha})=2\cos^2{\alpha}-1 &&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}})\\ &=(1-\sin^2{\alpha})-\sin^2{\alpha}=1-2\sin^2{\alpha}&&({\text{Trig Identity \#1}}) \tag*{$\blacksquare$} \end{align*}cos2ќ±вАЛ=cos(ќ±+ќ±)=cosќ±cosќ±вИТsinќ±sinќ±=cos2ќ±вИТsin2ќ±=cos2ќ±вИТ(1вИТcos2ќ±)=2cos2ќ±вИТ1=(1вИТsin2ќ±)вИТsin2ќ±=1вИТ2sin2ќ±вАЛвАЛ(Trig Identity #6)(Trig Identity #1)(Trig Identity #1)вАЛвЦ†вАЛ Trig Identity #6 Trig Identity #1

Fundamental Principle of Counting Suppose nnn decisions are made in sequence, where at the iiith step there are kik_ikiвАЛ available options. If each decision is made independently (meaning the outcome of one will not influence or be influenced by any other), then the total number of unique ways to perform all nnn decisions is вИПi=1nki=k1√Чk2√ЧвЛѓ√Чkn\prod_{i=1}^{n}k_{i}=k_1\times k_2\times \cdots \times k_ni=1вИПnвАЛkiвАЛ=k1вАЛ√Чk2вАЛ√ЧвЛѓ√ЧknвАЛ

There are four counting methods, depending on two questions: does order matter? And can choices repeat? When order matters, we call it Permutations; on the contrary, when order does not matters, we call it Combinations. When choices can repeat, we say the selection is with Replacement; on the contrary, when choices cannot repeat, we call it without Replacement.

Permutations with replacement If there are nnn unique items, then the number of Permutations to select kkk items with replacement is: nkn^k nk Derivation By the Fundamental Principle of Counting, this is just kkk independent steps, where at each step there are nnn choices avaliable (since replacement means there is nothing moved from the pool). Therefore, the number of Permutations is : n√Чn√ЧвЛѓ√ЧnвПЯktimes=nkвЦ†\underbrace{n\times n\times \cdots \times n}_{k\text{times}}=n^k \tag*{$\blacksquare$}ktimesn√Чn√ЧвЛѓ√ЧnвАЛвАЛ=nkвЦ†

Permutations without Replacement If there are nnn unique items, then the number of Permutations to select kkk items without replacement is: n!(nвИТk)!\frac{n!}{(n-k)!}(nвИТk)!n!вАЛ Derivation By the Fundamental Principle of Counting, this is just kkk independent steps, but since items are not replaced, the pool shrinks by one at each step: nnn choice in step 1, nвИТ1n-1nвИТ1 choice in step two, and so on down to nвИТk+1n-k+1nвИТk+1 in step kkk. Therefore, the number of Permutations is : n√Ч(nвИТ1)√Ч(nвИТ2)√ЧвЛѓ√Ч(nвИТk+1)=n!(nвИТk)!вЦ†n\times (n-1)\times (n-2)\times \cdots\times (n-k+1) =\frac{n!}{(n-k)!} \tag*{$\blacksquare$}n√Ч(nвИТ1)√Ч(nвИТ2)√ЧвЛѓ√Ч(nвИТk+1)=(nвИТk)!n!вАЛвЦ†

Combinations without Replacement If there are nnn unique items, then the number of Combinations to select kkk items without replacement is denoted (nk)\binom{n}{k}(knвАЛ), read as "n choose k", and defined as: (nk)=defn!k!(nвИТk)!\binom{n}{k}\overset{\text{def}}{=}\frac{n!}{k!(n-k)!}(knвАЛ)=defk!(nвИТk)!n!вАЛ This could also be found in [Binomial Theorem] Derivation Start with permutation without Replacement, and the number of Permutations to select kkk items from nnn without replacement is: n!(nвИТk)!\frac{n!}{(n-k)!}(nвИТk)!n!вАЛ But since order doesn't matter, every group of kkk items gets counted k!k!k! times, so we divide out the overcounting and get: n!(nвИТk)!√Јk!=n!k!(nвИТk)!=(nk)вЦ†\frac{n!}{(n-k)!}\div {k!}=\frac{n!}{k!(n-k)!}=\binom{n}{k} \tag*{$\blacksquare$}(nвИТk)!n!вАЛ√Јk!=k!(nвИТk)!n!вАЛ=(knвАЛ)вЦ†

To use the classical definition of probability, we usually follow three steps: first identify the sample space ќ©\Omegaќ©, then describe the event AAA, and finally count the number of outcomes in each set. The following example shows how these definitions work together. Rolling a Die A fair six-sided die is rolled once; what is the probability of rolling an even number? Solution Since it is a six-sided die, the sample sapce is: ќ©={1,2,3,4,5,6}\Omega=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}ќ©={1,2,3,4,5,6} Let AAA be the event of rolling an even number: A={2,4,6}A=\{2,4,6\}A={2,4,6} Then: вИ£AвИ£=3,вИ£ќ©вИ£=6|A|=3, \qquad |\Omega|=6вИ£AвИ£=3,вИ£ќ©вИ£=6 Since all outcomes are equally likely, by the classical definition of probability: P(A)=вИ£AвИ£вИ£ќ©вИ£=36=12P(A)=\frac{|A|}{|\Omega|}=\frac{3}{6}=\frac{1}{2}P(A)=вИ£ќ©вИ£вИ£AвИ£вАЛ=63вАЛ=21вАЛ

As we warned before, the classical definition of probability only applies when the sample space is finite and all outcomes are equally likely, and here is a counter example. Circle in a Square A circle is inscribed in a square with side length 1. If a point is chosen uniformly at random inside the square, what is the probability that the point lies inside the circle? False Attempt When attempt the classical definition of probability: P(A)=вИ£AвИ£вИ£ќ©вИ£P(A)=\frac{|A|}{|\Omega|}P(A)=вИ£ќ©вИ£вИ£AвИ£вАЛ where AAA is the set of points inside the circle and ќ©\Omegaќ© is the set of all points inside the square. However, both AAA and ќ©\Omegaќ© contain infinitely many points, so we cannot compute the probability by counting outcomes, as: P(A)=вИ£AвИ£вИ£ќ©вИ£=вИЮвИЮ=вЭМP(A)=\frac{|A|}{|\Omega|}=\frac{\infty}{\infty}={вЭМ}P(A)=вИ£ќ©вИ£вИ£AвИ£вАЛ=вИЮвИЮвАЛ=вЭМ This example shows why probability needs a more general definition. Instead of counting equally likely outcomes, we want probability to assign a consistent вАЬsizeвАЭ to events, whether those events are finite sets or infinite sets.

If ќ©\Omegaќ© is the sample sapce, then every event AAA satisfy: AвКЖќ©A \subseteq \OmegaAвКЖќ© Hence, instead of only counting individual outcomes, we can study how events relate to each other as sets. UnionвАЛ Union The union of two events AAA and BBB, denoted by AвИ™BA\cup BAвИ™B, is the event either AAA or BBB happensпЉМalso refered to "AAA or BBB". IntersectionвАЛ Intersection The intersection of two events AAA and BBB, denoted by AвИ©BA\cap BAвИ©B, is the event both AAA and BBB happens, also refered to "AAA and BBB". In 1933, Russian mathematician Andrey Kolmogorov published Foundations of the Theory of Probability, which laid the modern axiomatic foundations of probability theory. Let's discover Docusaurus in less than 5 minutes.
Get started by creating a new site. Or try Docusaurus immediately with docusaurus.new.
Node.js version 20.0 or above: When installing Node.js, you are recommended to check all checkboxes related to dependencies.

Generate a new Docusaurus site using the classic template. The classic template will automatically be added to your project after you run the command: npm init docusaurus@latest my-website classic You can type this command into Command Prompt, Powershell, Terminal, or any other integrated terminal of your code editor. The command also installs all necessary dependencies you need to run Docusaurus.

Run the development server: cd my-website npm run start The cd command changes the directory you're working with. In order to work with your newly created Docusaurus site, you'll need to navigate the terminal there. The npm run start command builds your website locally and serves it through a development server, ready for you to view at http://localhost:3000/. Open docs/intro.md (this page) and edit some lines: the site reloads automatically and displays your changes.
